behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the key-derivation function itself (canonicalization, hashing/serialization). No DB/network/OS dependency is required to expose real bugs like order sensitivity, type normalization errors, or unstable serialization. A pure unit/property-based test can catch these directly.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the real key builder with table-driven and property-based inputs: same params in different map orders must produce identical keys; intentionally different params (value/type/field changes) must produce different keys across a large randomized corpus; include edge cases like nulls, arrays, unicode, and numeric/string distinctions.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: Real failures often come from wiring between config parsing and TTL computation (unit mismatches, missing category fallback, stale config object), which a mocked config in a pure unit test can miss. No external OS/network dependency is required, so Integration is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test loading the real freshness configuration path (same parser/format used in production) and the TTL calculator with a fixed clock. Assert category-to-TTL mappings (e.g., user_profiles=300s, product_catalog=3600s), unknown-category behavior, and unit correctness (minutes vs seconds).

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: The risk is module interaction: memory-pressure signal + threshold config + strategy selector state transition. A unit test of only selector logic can miss integration bugs (wrong metric source, wrong threshold units, missed transition). This does not require real OS memory behavior if the in-process memory monitor implementation is exercised.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with cache manager, real strategy-selector wiring, and real in-process memory tracker implementation. Drive cache usage across the configured threshold and verify active policy changes from LRU to LFU (and optional hysteresis behavior if implemented). Validate config-driven threshold interpretation.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real DB write semantics and cross-process/event behavior (transaction commit timing, trigger/listener/query invalidation path). In-process mocks can hide failures in SQL, notification wiring, and commit-order races. This crosses into subprocess/external service behavior.
behaviors.test_description: System test against a real PostgreSQL instance (ephemeral container/local service) with the production invalidation mechanism enabled. Seed cache entries, perform INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on target rows, commit transaction, and assert matching cache keys are invalidated while non-matching keys remain. Include at least one multi-row write case.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Eviction correctness depends on interaction between cache storage, access/update bookkeeping, and policy implementation. A mock-heavy unit test may miss real bookkeeping bugs (touch not recorded, frequency counter drift, insertion/update semantics). No external system is required.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with real cache store + LRU/LFU policy modules at small fixed capacity. Execute deterministic access patterns to create known recency/frequency orderings, then insert new items to force eviction. Assert exact keys evicted for LRU and LFU modes, including tie-breaker behavior defined by spec.
